observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the 2024 bank reconciliation statements for all cash in bank accounts and ensure that the reports will be regularly submitted then;
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit also the supporting documents of the BRS such as Bank Statements and Subsidiary Ledger; and
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to analyze the cash variance in bank accounts, prepare and submit adjusting entries, and reconcile their records regularly.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct:
Ensure the application of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts; and
Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare the Physical Inventory Plan to be approved by the Local Chief Executive, and submit it to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to-
Require the Municipal Treasurer Office to immediately furnish the Accounting Office with all pending RCDs, including deposit slips, for proper recording and reconciliation;
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all collections and deposits are recorded promptly in the General Ledger; and
Implement monthly reconciliation between the Treasurer’s Cashbook and the General Ledger to detect and resolve discrepancies early.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a comprehensive reconciliation of the Due to BIR, Due to GSIS, and Due to PhilHealth accounts to determine the underlying cause of the negative balances;
Effect necessary adjustments and ensure that liability accounts accurately reflect outstanding obligations. Any mis posted or overstated amounts should be reversed or reclassified appropriately to prevent further financial misstatements; and
Submit the Subsidiary Ledger of the liability accounts.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entries to recognize the Trust Liability – DRRMF representing the unexpended LDRRM funds. Strictly comply with the following journal entries relevant to transfer of unutilized LDRRMF;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer unutilized portion of CY 2024 Quick Response Fund (QRF) to the LDRRMF bank account of the Municipality; and
Require the MDRRMO to submit the required monthly fund utilization reports of DRRMF to COA Audit Team using the prescribed format.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Facilitate immediate submission of the unsubmitted accounts to avoid audit suspension thereof;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to approve and process only those disbursement vouchers supported with complete and valid documentation and approvals; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the paid DVs and payrolls on or before 10th day of the ensuing month.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) every month for every infrastructure project implemented by administration; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the following documents for each project: Inspection Report by the Agency’s Authorized Engineer, Pictures, before, during and after construction of items of work especially the embedded items and Certificate of Completion.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documents not later than March 30, 2025 to avoid audit suspension thereof; and
Ensure that only those disbursement vouchers supported with complete and valid documentations and approvals will be approved and processed.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require all Department Heads or implementing units to prepare their respective Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMPs) for their different programs, activities and projects (PAPs) in the format prescribed by the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) and linkage to the approved annual budget;
Direct the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the PPMPs into Annual Procurement Plan (APP) in the format prescribed by GPPB Circular 08-2015 and to submit the report independent of the annual budget of the Municipality; and
Require all the BAC Chairman, BAC members and BAC Secretariat to attend relevant trainings and seminars on procurement planning and budgeting.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare and submit regularly the Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPBB and Commission on Audit (COA) at the end of each semester and to observe the same in the years thereafter.

We recommended that the Chief Local Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and BAC Chairman to send written invitations or notices to the Commission on Audit at least five (5) calendar days before the scheduled public biddings, as required by the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the general fund for the 20% Development Fund and supported by a separate set of subsidiary ledgers for each development project charged to the said fund, whether current or continuing, and prepare financial statements exclusive for the 20% DF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the LDRRMO to include in the LDRRMFIP the list of programs, projects, and activities charged to the prior year’s unexpended LDRRMF in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to:
Submit the bank reconciliation statements for CY 2023 for all cash in bank accounts and ensure that the reports will be regularly submitted then;
Submit also the supporting documents of the BRS such as Bank Statements and Subsidiary Ledger; and
Analyze the cash variance in bank accounts, prepare and submit adjusting entries, and reconcile with Municipal Treasurer’s records regularly.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to discontinue or minimize the practice of granting multiple cash advances on a particular day to the Disbursing Officers but instead pay directly under the name of the payees, concerned employees and suppliers;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record transactions that are cash advances in nature with the appropriate Advances account(s) applicable and not as outright expenses. Make the necessary adjusting entries; and
Direct the Disbursing Officers to submit liquidation reports with complete supporting documents of their cash advances as of December 31, 2023 within 15 days from receipt hereof, otherwise the transactions shall be suspended pursuant to COA Circular 2009-006 prescribing the rules and regulations on settlement of accounts.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry recognizing the trust liability for the unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund (QRF) and the DRRMF-MOOE in the Trust Fund books and submit subsidiary ledger of the account. Strictly comply with the following journal entries relevant to transfer of unutilized LDRRMF:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe prompt transfer or deposit to the Trust Fund of the unutilized portion of LDRRMF Quick Response Fund (QRF) and DRRMF-MOOE;
Require the MDRRMO to submit Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis; and
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit SAOB for the Current and Continuing Appropriation.

Strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances set forth under COA Circular No. 97-002, refraining from granting cash advances without submission of the complete supporting documents by the disbursing officer and from granting multiple cash advances; requiring immediate submission of liquidation reports with complete supporting documents as prescribed under COA Circular 2012-001, ensuring that the required time frame for liquidation of cash advances is observed and that the cash advance for payroll fund shall be equal to the net amount of the payroll for the pay period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the preparation and submission of CY 2020 Bank Reconciliation Statements for General Fund immediately.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of the local roads under the jurisdiction of the municipality and render a Report on the Physical count of the Road Networks System;
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Designated Municipal General Service Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
Instruct the Designated Municipal General Service Officer to render a Report on Local Road Network and Road Map and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the municipal roads and its components based on the appraisal of the Municipal Engineer and to maintain
property ledger cards for each infrastructure for proper monitoring of transactions.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive appropriate fund for the titling of all land; direct the Municipal Assessor̕ s Office to exert effort in titling the land owned by the Municipality; and direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain property and subsidiary ledger cards.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately all disbursement vouchers and to approve and process only those disbursement vouchers supported with complete and valid documentations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Discontinue or minimize the practice of granting multiple cash advances on a particular day to the Municipal Treasurer; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Disbursements from January to December 2020.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to update all her cashbooks regularly for proper determination of accountabilities; to maintain Check Register and; to prepare and submit List of Unreleased Checks on a monthly basis along with the Report of Checks Issued.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to undertake controls and strategies in order to ensure adherence with the required time frame for the submission of monthly trial balance, quarterly financial statements and other reports.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit the latest Status of Appropriations, Allotments, and Obligations (SAOB) for CY 2020 and to ensure that Obligation Requests are properly accomplished.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure that the list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented for the year will be submitted every beginning of the year; and to inform COA within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the PPAs that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC Secretariat to submit all approved contracts together with its supporting papers and to ensure that prospective contracts are submitted within 5 days upon its perfection.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct all Department heads to prepare and to submit their individual PPMPs showing the detailed list of goods or infrastructure projects to be procured during the coming year to the BAC Secretariat and to the Municipal Budget Officer who will ensure that amount involved are within budget; and
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare and submit regularly the Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPBB and Commission on Audit (COA) within fourteen (14) calendar days after end of each semester.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive fully institutionalize the MDRRM Office by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions particularly the MDDRO II position in order not to affect the LGU capability to respond during calamities.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Designated MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit a revise Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF indicating therein all funds expended for calamity in CY 2020 and to submit calamity report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Designated MDDRMO and Municipal Accountant to prepare the reports, post the same to appropriate website, bulletin board or conspicuous places, and to submit the Annex C of DBM LBC Circular No. 125 to COA.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit Bank Reconciliation Statements  for all bank accounts for the period of CY 2019; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to initiate the closure of the PNB bank account and to conduct review/analyze other bank accounts with no transactions in order to close the same, if warranted.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to transfer the property, plant and equipment including the two projects under the Construction In Progress account from Trust Fund books to the General Fund books and to reconcile and correct the government equity account balance.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer to delegate the real property tax collections to the other Revenue Collectors to reduce her workload and to ensure that all her collections as well as the collections of the three Revenue Collectors were deposited intact and deposited in the bank in the frequency required under existing regulation.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer the following:
Stop the practice of using cash advance to pay for the Municipality’s operating expenses particularly for GAD and LCPC activities;
Ensure that disbursements should be issued with check directly to the name of the payees, concerned employees, and suppliers; and
Submit all Report of Disbursements for CY 2019.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to facilitate the adoption of ATM or Payroll-thru-bank system to avoid hold-ups and similar incidents involving payrolls and to promote operational efficiency.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to furnish the Accounting office with a copy of Schedule of Earned Leave Credits with money value for all LGU personnel as basis by the Accounting Office to record the leave accruals for those with budget and in the meantime to disclose the unrecorded liabilities in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit immediately reports and ensure to strictly adhere with the required time frame for the submission of accounts.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare RAAF and CRAAF and submit to COA the said monthly reports in the prescribed form provided in the NGAS Manual not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain records and subsidiary ledger for each barangay to facilitate the release of RPT within the prescribed period; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately release the ₱12,039.75 shares from RPT and CTC to the different barangays as well as the P16,855.60 due to the Province of Lanao del Norte.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive require the BAC Secretariat to submit to COA the CY 2019 approved contracts together with its supporting papers and to ensure that prospective all contracts are submitted within 5 days upon its perfection.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure compliance with RA 9184, its IRR and related issuance in future procurement transactions particularly on posting of the Annual Procurement Plan in the GPPB website, providing a copy of the APP in the disbursement voucher; to provide specifications in the Purchase Request; to submit proof of invitation to observers; to stop the reference of brand name, and to submit post qualification report and BAC resolution whenever the alternative method of procurement will be adopted.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to process disbursement vouchers related to procurement transactions only when supporting documents as required in RA 9184 are complied with to preclude the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly follow the procedures in Section 52 of RA 9184 or Shopping as alternative mode of procurement and to attach a request for quotation and abstract of canvass as required in Section 9.2.4 of COA Circular 2012-001; and
Require the General Supply Office and BAC to consider first procurement of ordinary office supplies and equipment at PS DBM or to its depots in compliance with Section 1 of Administrative Order No. 17 in order to avail of the price that is most advantage to the government.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documents not later than June 30, 2020; and
Ensure that only those disbursement vouchers supported with complete and valid documentations and approvals will be approved and processed

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant to ensure that the Calamity funds- QRF are utilized in accordance to NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JC 2013-01; and
Require the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Municipal Officer to maintain the required three (3) registries for calamity funds.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Ensure that expenditures charged to SEF are in accordance with the approved SEF Annual Budget; and
Direct the Local School Board to prepare and submit Annual SEF Budget with the prescribed form and timeline pursuant to the new Joint Circular Dep Ed-DBM and DILG No. 1 s. 2017.

Require the GAD Focal Person and MPDC to conduct gender mainstreaming to identify the appropriate activities responsive to the needs of the LGU constituents;
Establish and maintain a GAD Database in implementing the Magna Carta for Women which helps in formulating GAD-related activities based on information gathered; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to -
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to –
prepare an adjusting entry for the staled checks to bring the cash in bank account in its correct balance.
submit the Bank Reconciliation Statement under Trust Fund
give utmost priority and close supervision in the preparation of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements to ensure timely submission.
Request the Local Chief Executive to augment the work force assigned in the Accounting Office to facilitate timely recording and preparation of reports.

We recommended and management agreed to -
Reconcile these dormant accounts to its related projects to establish whether the remaining balance represent LGU equity or that from the source/funding agency in order to determine the appropriate action.
Undertake the necessary procedures for the closure of these dormant accounts to avoid further loss due to dormancy fees.

We recommended and management agreed to hire a Certified Public Accountant if plausible, and if not to hire or designate a personnel who has financial competence or skill to assume the office of the accounting section/department for a timely submission of the required accounting reports to COA.
During the exit conference, management informed the Audit Team that the LGU has a new Municipal Accountant effective March 2018.

We recommended and management agreed to -
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that payments for suppliers are made thru checks.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to attach supporting documents at the time of grant of cash advance.
Consider the designation of other Special Disbursing Officer (SDO) and establishment of Petty Cash to address petty expenditures of the LGU.

We recommended and management agreed to -
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately all lacking reports, disbursement vouchers together with its supporting documents and ensure to strictly adhere with the required time frame for the submission of accounts.
As reiterated, instruct management to hire a Certified Public Accountant if plausible, and if not to hire or designate a personnel who has financial competence or skill to assume the office of the accounting section/department for a timely submission of the required accounting reports to COA.

We recommended and management agreed to direct all Revenue Collectors to prepare the RAAF and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the CRAAF and submit the said monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms in the prescribed form provided in the NGAS Manual and submit the same not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month.

We recommended and management agreed to -
Direct all Accountable Officers to prepare and submit Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) on a daily basis or whenever there is transaction.
Advise the Municipal Treasurer to prepare RCD for each fund duly supported with the RCDs of the collectors and to properly accomplish the report.

We recommended and management agreed to -
Require the Municipal Treasurer
to provide official cashbooks to record all collections and disbursements for each type of fund and for each bank accounts in the case of cash in bank.
to submit the cashbook –cash in bank for all bank accounts immediately.
to accomplish the cashbook appropriately and completely.
Direct the Local Chief Executive to designate a Liquidating Officer to handle the remittances of the LGU Revenue Collectors and the deposit of said funds.

We recommended and management agreed to -
Require the BAC Secretariat –
to submit to COA the approved contracts together with its supporting papers for the said perfected projects for COA’s review.
to submit prospective contracts within 5 days upon its perfection in accordance with COA Circular 2009-001.
Advise management headed by the Local Chief Executive to revisit the internal control policies over LGU operations to identify and address these recurring lapses over submission of contracts.

We recommended and management agreed to -
Direct the BAC Chairman and BAC Secretariat to require all Department Heads to include in their PPMP items necessary in their respective programs/activities.
Require all members of the Bids and Awards Committee and the Head of the Procuring Entity to attend seminars/trainings that will improve their knowledge in the procurement.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive to:
Make representation with the National Solid Waste Management Commission for the approval of the plan; and
Conversion of the open dumpsite into a controlled dumpsite.

We recommended and management agreed to -
Direct the Local School Board to
prepare and submit Annual SEF Budget with the prescribed form and timeline pursuant to the new Joint Circular Dep Ed-DBM and DILG No. 1 s. 2017.
ensure that SEF funds are released and utilized pursuant to Section 272 of  RA 7160.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the Local Chief Executive to instruct the GAD Focal Person
to conduct gender mainstreaming to identify the appropriate activities responsive to the needs of the LGU constituents.
to prepare GAD Plan and Budget using the prescribed format under JMC 2013- 01 of  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA.
